docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
• $ : represent shell with regular user
  • # : represent shell with root user

influx

docs.title: MariaDB
docs.text: This section covers various commands and procedures for managing MariaDB.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
Installs MariaDB and runs the secure installation script. 

mariadb_secure_install

docs.title: Root password
docs.text: This subsection provides methods for changing the root password in MariaDB.

docs.title: Hot change
docs.text:
Changes the root password without restarting the MariaDB service. 

mysqladmin -u root -p'OLDPASSWORD' password 'NEWPASSWORD';

docs.title: Cold change
docs.text:
Changes the root password after stopping and starting the MariaDB service. 

systemctl mariadb stop
mysqld_safe --skip-grant-tables &
mysql mysql -u root
UPDATE user SET password=PASSWORD('NEWPASSWORD') WHERE user="root";
FLUSH PRIVILEGES;
exit
systemctl mariadb start

docs.title: User split
docs.text:
Creates a new database and grants privileges to a user. 

mysqladmin -u root -p create MYDB
mysql -u root -p mysql
GRANT ALL ON MYDB.* TO MYUSER@'%' IDENTIFIED BY 'NEWPASSWORD';
GRANT ALL ON MYDB.* TO MYUSER@localhost IDENTIFIED BY 'NEWPASSWORD';
FLUSH PRIVILEGES;
exit
* % : from any ip * localhost: only from localhost

docs.title: Extract permissions
docs.text:
Displays the SQL commands needed to recreate the grants for each user. 

use mysql
select distinct concat('SHOW GRANTS FOR ''' , user, '''@''',host,''';') as query from user;

docs.title: Tables
docs.text: This subsection provides commands related to database tables.

docs.title: Copy
docs.text:
Creates a new table by copying an existing one. 

CREATE TABLE new_table_name AS
SELECT * FROM old_table_name;

docs.title: Log requests
docs.text: This subsection explains how to log MariaDB requests.

docs.title: Activate
docs.text:
Enables the general query log. 

SET global general_log = 1;

docs.title: Destination
docs.text: Specifies where the general query log should be stored.

docs.title: Table
docs.text:
SET global log_output = 'table';

select * from general_log;
select * from mysql.general_log where command_type = 'Query' and user_host = 'user[user] @ localhost []'

docs.title: File
docs.text:
SET global log_output = 'file';
SET global general_log_file = '/tmp/mysql_trace.log';

docs.title: Deactivate
docs.text:
Disables the general query log. 

SET global general_log = 0;

docs.title: Upgrade
docs.text:
This subsection explains how to upgrade MariaDB.

# service mariadb stop
# yum remove MariaDB-*
# cat /etc/yum.repos.d/MariaDB.repo
# sed -i 's/11\.1/11\.2/' /etc/yum.repos.d/MariaDB.repo
# yum install MariaDB-server
# service mariadb start
# /usr/bin/mariadb-upgrade -u root -p

# apt install mongodb-org
# systemctl enable --now mongod

docs.title: Create admin first
docs.text:
$ mongosh admin
switched to db admin

db.createUser(
  {
    user: "admin",
    pwd: "admin_pwd",
    roles: [
      { role: "userAdminAnyDatabase", db: "admin" },
      { role: "readWriteAnyDatabase", db: "admin" }
    ]
  }
)

...

docs.title: Login
docs.text:
• failed 
    $ mongosh
test> db.getCollectionNames()
MongoServerError: Command listCollections requires authentication
  • success 
    $ mongosh mongodb://admin:admin_pwd@localhost:27017/test --authenticationDatabase admin
test> db.getCollectionNames()
[]

docs.title: get
docs.text:
$ mongosh mongodb://admin:admin_pwd@localhost:27017/test --authenticationDatabase admin
admin> db.getUsers()
{
  users: [
    {
      _id: 'admin.admin',
      userId: new UUID("58625790-8e6b-4433-9e54-777ede29afea"),
      user: 'admin',
      db: 'admin',
      roles: [
        { role: 'readWriteAnyDatabase', db: 'admin' },
        { role: 'userAdminAnyDatabase', db: 'admin' }
      ],
      mechanisms: [ 'SCRAM-SHA-1', 'SCRAM-SHA-256' ]
    }
  ],
  ok: 1
}

docs.title: Add role
docs.text:
admin> db.grantRolesToUser('admin',['dbAdminAnyDatabase'])
{ ok: 1 }
admin> db.getUsers()
{
  users: [
    {
      _id: 'admin.admin',
      userId: new UUID("58625790-8e6b-4433-9e54-777ede29afea"),
      user: 'admin',
      db: 'admin',
      roles: [
        { role: 'dbAdminAnyDatabase', db: 'admin' },
        { role: 'userAdminAnyDatabase', db: 'admin' },
        { role: 'readWriteAnyDatabase', db: 'admin' }
      ],
      mechanisms: [ 'SCRAM-SHA-1', 'SCRAM-SHA-256' ]
    }
  ],
  ok: 1
}

docs.title: Change password
docs.text:
admin> db.changeUserPassword("svc_backup","mysqlpwd")
{ ok: 1 }

docs.text:
$ mongosh 
test> use ex_db
switched to db ex_db
ex_db> db.createCollection("posts")

ex_db> show dbs
admin   116.00 KiB
config  108.00 KiB

docs.title: Drop
docs.text:
• User must have role : dbAdmin or dbAdminAnyDatabase 
    $ mongosh 
test> use ex_db
switched to db ex_db
ex_db> db.dropDatabase()
{ ok: 1, dropped: 'ex_db' }
ex_db> show dbs
admin   116.00 KiB
config  108.00 KiB
local    72.00 KiB

docs.title: Create
docs.text:
use master 
CREATE LOGIN clinux WITH PASSWORD = 'Clinux';

IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT * FROM sys.databases WHERE name = 'db_clinux')
BEGIN
CREATE DATABASE db_clinux;
END;
GO

use db_clinux
CREATE USER u_clinux FOR LOGIN clinux;
ALTER ROLE db_owner ADD MEMBER u_clinux ;
GO

docs.title: Basic ssh,http,https
docs.text:
nft add table inet firewall
nft add chain inet firewall input { type filter hook input priority 0 \; policy drop\; } 
nft add rule inet firewall input ct state established,related accept
nft add rule inet firewall input ct state invalid drop
nft add rule inet firewall input ip protocol icmp limit rate 4/second accept
nft add rule inet firewall input tcp dport { 22, http, https }
nft add rule inet firewall input udp dport { 22, http, https }
# don't forward anything, you're not a router!
nft add chain inet firewall forward { type filter hook forward priority 0 \; policy drop\; } 
# allow all outgoing traffic
nft add chain inet firewall output { type filter hook output priority 0 \; policy accept\; }

docs.title: create test
docs.text:
---
- name : test user
  hosts: bpg-debian12
  remote_user: debian
  become: true

  tasks:
  - name: get id
    shell: id
    register: results
  - name: Add the user 'clinux'
    ansible.builtin.user:
      name: clinux
      comment: created by ansible
  - name: break
    shell: exit 1
    ignore_errors: true
  - name: Remove the user 'clinux'
    ansible.builtin.user:
      name: clinux
      state: absent
      remove: yes

  - name: Install some packages
    package:
      name:
        - htop
        - curl
        - nmon
        - net-tools
        - nginx
      state: latest

  - name: Make sure a service unit is running
    ansible.builtin.systemd_service:
      state: started
      enabled: true
      name: nginx

  - debug:
      var: results.stdout

docs.title: create post tofu root
docs.text:
---
- name: "tofu : setup root key"
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    remote_src: true
    src: /home/debian/.ssh/authorized_keys
    dest: /root/.ssh/authorized_keys
    owner: root
    group: root
    mode: '0600'

- name: "tofu : allow root sshkey"
  lineinfile:
    path: /etc/ssh/sshd_config
    regexp: '(.*)PermitRootLogin(.*)$'
    line: 'PermitRootLogin without-password'
    owner: root

- name: "tofu : reload ssh"
  service:
    name: ssh
    state: restarted

docs.title: Execute
docs.text:
ansible-playbook plb_get_user.yml -i inventory
export ANSIBLE_INVENTORY=inventory
ansible-playbook plb_get_user.yml

docs.title: galaxy
docs.text: ansible-galaxy init roles/post_tofu --offline

docs.title: structure
docs.text: mkdir -p roles/post_tofu/tasks

docs.title: main
docs.text:
cat << EOF > roles/post_tofu/tasks/main.yml
- include_tasks: copy_ssh_key_root.yml
EOF

docs.title: task sshkey
docs.text:
cat << EOF > roles/post_tofu/tasks/copy_ssh_key_root.yml
---
- name: copy ssh authozied_keys to root
  ansible.builtin.copy:
    src: /home/debian/.ssh/authorized_keys
    dest: /root/.ssh/authorized_keys
    remote_src: yes
    owner: root
    group: root

- name: Update and upgrade apt packages
  apt:
    upgrade: yes
    update_cache: yes
    cache_valid_time: 86400 #One day
EOF

docs.title: playbook
docs.text:
cat << EOF > deploy_post_tofu.yml
---
- name : post actions tofu
  hosts: debian12
  remote_user: debian
  become: true

  roles:
    - post_tofu
EOF

docs.title: run
docs.text: ansible-playbook deploy_post_tofu.yml

docs.title: structure
docs.text: mkdir -p roles/nginx/{tasks,handlers,files}

docs.title: tasks
docs.text:
cat << EOF > roles/nginx/tasks/main.yml

---
- include_tasks: install_packages.yml
- include_tasks: configuration.yml
EOF

cat << EOF > roles/nginx/tasks/install_packages.yml

---
- name: Install nginx
  package:
    name:
      - nginx
      - net-tools
    state: latest

- name: Make sure a service unit is running
  ansible.builtin.systemd_service:
    state: started
    enabled: true
    name: nginx
EOF

cat << EOF > roles/nginx/tasks/configuration.yml
- name: copy configuration
  copy:
    src: nginx.conf
    dest: /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
    owner: root
    group: root
  notify: restart_nginx
  tags:
    - configuration_nginx
EOF

docs.title: handlers
docs.text:
cat << EOF > roles/nginx/handlers/main.yml
- name: Restart nginx
  service:
    name: nginx
    state: restarted
  listen: restart_nginx
EOF

docs.title: files
docs.text:
cat << EOF > roles/nginx/files/nginx.conf
user www-data;
worker_processes auto;
pid /run/nginx.pid;
error_log /var/log/nginx/error.log;
include /etc/nginx/modules-enabled/*.conf;

events {
    worker_connections 768;
    # multi_accept on;
}

http {

    ##
    # Basic Settings
    ##

    sendfile on;
    tcp_nopush on;
    types_hash_max_size 2048;
    # server_tokens off;

    server_names_hash_bucket_size 64;
    # server_name_in_redirect off;

    include /etc/nginx/mime.types;
    default_type application/octet-stream;

    ##
    # SSL Settings
    ##

    ssl_protocols TLSv1 TLSv1.1 TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3; # Dropping SSLv3, ref: POODLE
    ssl_prefer_server_ciphers on;

    ##
    # Logging Settings
    ##

    access_log /var/log/nginx/access_default.log;

    ##
    # Gzip Settings
    ##

    gzip on;

    gzip_vary on;
    gzip_proxied any;
    gzip_comp_level 6;
    gzip_buffers 16 8k;
    gzip_http_version 1.1;
    gzip_types text/plain text/css application/json application/javascript text/xml application/xml application/xml+rss text/javascript;

    ##
    # Virtual Host Configs
    ##

    include /etc/nginx/conf.d/*.conf;
    include /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/*;
}
EOF

docs.title: Add role
docs.text:
pveum role add OpenTofu -privs "Datastore.Allocate Datastore.AllocateSpace Datastore.AllocateTemplate Datastore.Audit Pool.Allocate Sys.Audit Sys.Console Sys.Modify SDN.Use VM.Allocate VM.Audit VM.Clone VM.Config.CDROM VM.Config.Cloudinit VM.Config.CPU VM.Config.Disk VM.Config.HWType VM.Config.Memory VM.Config.Network VM.Config.Options VM.Migrate VM.Monitor VM.PowerMgmt User.Modify"

or

Use PVEAdmin

docs.title: link role user
docs.text: pveum aclmod / -user opentofu@pve -role OpenTofu

docs.title: generate token
docs.text: pveum user token add opentofu@pve opentofu -expire 0 -privsep 0 -comment "OpenTofu token"

docs.title: test token
docs.text: curl -X GET 'https://$PROXMOX_URL:8006/api2/json/nodes' -H 'Authorization: PVEAPIToken=opentofu@pve!opentofu=$TOKEN'

docs.title: provider.tf
docs.text:
terraform {
    required_providers {
        proxmox =  {
        source = "bpg/proxmox"
        version = ">= 0.38.1"
        }
    }
}
provider "proxmox" {
    endpoint = var.pm_api_url
    api_token = var.pm_api_token
    insecure = false  # Warning TLS cert of proxmox webui must be valid (Let's Encrypt)
    ssh {
        agent    = true
        username = "root"
    }
}

docs.title: vars.tf
docs.text:
variable  "pm_api_url" {
    default =  "https://$PROXMOX_URL:8006/api2/json"
}
variable  "pm_api_token" {
    default =  "opentofu@pve!opentofu=XXXXXXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXX-XXXXXXXXXXXX"
}
variable  "target_node" {
    default =  "proxmox2"
}
variable  "ssh_key" {
    default =  "ssh-ed25519 XXXXXXXX clinux@rocky9.local"
}

docs.title: envvars
docs.text:
export PROXMOX_VE_ENDPOINT='https://PROXMOX_URL:8006/'
export PROXMOX_VE_API_TOKEN='USER@REMAM!TOKEN_KEY=TOKEN_VALUE'

docs.title: provider.tf
docs.text:
terraform {
    required_providers {
        proxmox =  {
        source = "bpg/proxmox"
        version = ">= 0.38.1"
        }
    }
}
provider "proxmox" {
    insecure = false  # Warning TLS cert of proxmox webui must be valid (Let's Encrypt)
}

docs.title: main.tf
docs.text:
resource "proxmox_virtual_environment_vm" "debian_vm" {
    name        = "bpg-debian12"
    description = "Managed by opentofu"
    tags        = ["opentofu", "debian"]
    node_name = var.target_node
    clone {
        vm_id = "1000"
    }

    cpu {
        cores = 2
        type = "host"
        numa = true
    }
    memory {
        dedicated = 2048
    }
    network_device {
        bridge = "vmbr0"
        model = "virtio"
    }

    efi_disk {
        datastore_id = "local-lvm"
        file_format = "raw"
        type    = "4m"
    }

    disk {
        datastore_id = "local-lvm"
        file_format = "raw"
        interface = "scsi0"
        size = "10"
    }

    operating_system {
        type = "l26"
    }
    machine = "q35"
    agent {
        enabled = false
    }

    initialization {
        ip_config {
            ipv4 {
                address = "192.168.1.170/24"
                gateway = "192.168.1.1"
            }
        }
        user_account {
            keys     = [var.ssh_key]
            password = "tofu"
            username = "debian"
        }
    }
}

docs.title: History
docs.text: This section provides an overview of commands and configurations related to the command history in a Unix/Linux shell.

docs.title: View
docs.text: This subsection explains how to view and search through the command history.

docs.title: list
docs.text:
Lists all previously executed commands. 

$ history

docs.title: search all
docs.text:
Explains how to search for specific commands in the history using grep and shortcuts. 

$ history | grep sudo
$ !sudo:p 
$ !18:p

docs.title: search interactive
docs.text:
Describes how to use interactive search to find commands in the history. 

Ctrl + R

docs.title: Action
docs.text: This subsection covers the actions that can be performed on the command history.

docs.title: execute
docs.text:
Shows how to re-execute specific commands from the history. 

$ !sudo
$ !18

docs.title: clean history
docs.text:
Explains how to clear the command history. 

$ history -cw
$ > ~/.bash_history

docs.title: config
docs.text: This subsection details the possible configurations for the command history.

docs.title: Add timestamp
docs.text:
Indicates how to add a timestamp to each command in the history. 

$ echo "export HISTTIMEFORMAT='%F, %T '" >> ~/.bashrc
$ source ~/.bashrc

docs.title: Increase history length (default 1000)
docs.text:
Explains how to increase the default command history length. 

$ echo "HISTSIZE=10000" >> ~/.bashrc
$ echo "HISTFILESIZE=10000" >> ~/.bashrc    
$ source ~/.bashrc

docs.title: Direct write to history
docs.text:
Shows how to configure direct writing of commands to the history file. 

$ echo "PROMPT_COMMAND='history -a'" >> ~/.bashrc   
$ source ~/.bashrc

docs.title: Listen port
docs.text: netstat -tnlpv

docs.title: Get host with icmp
docs.text: # nmap -sn 192.168.1.0/24

docs.title: install
docs.text: # apt install rkhunter

docs.title: run
docs.text:
# rkhunter -c --rwo
  • -c : check
  • --rwo : report warning only

docs.title: lynis
docs.text:
# cd /usr/local/sbin && git clone https://github.com/CISOfy/lynis
# lynis audit system

docs.title: Storage
docs.text: This section provides an overview of commands and configurations related to disk storage management.

docs.title: Rescan disk without bootting
docs.text: echo "- - -" | tee /sys/class/scsi_host/host*/scan

docs.title: Rescue disk
docs.text: dd if=/dev/sdc of=/home/mako/damaged_P300_disk.img bs=64K conv=noerror,sync

docs.title: Disk extension
docs.text: This subsection explains how to check and extend disk partitions.

docs.title: check
docs.text:
Checks the current disk and partition sizes. 

lsblk
Output example: 
NAME    MAJ:MIN RM  SIZE RO TYPE MOUNTPOINTS
sdb       8:16   0  110G  0 disk
└─sdb1    8:17   0  100G  0 part /rsync

docs.title: extend partition
docs.text:
Explains how to extend the partition size. 

growpart -N /dev/sdb 1
growpart /dev/sdb 1
Checks the updated partition sizes. 
lsblk
Output example: 
NAME    MAJ:MIN RM  SIZE RO TYPE MOUNTPOINTS
sdb       8:16   0  110G  0 disk
└─sdb1    8:17   0  110G  0 part /rsync
Checks the file system disk space usage. 
df -h
Output example: 
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/sdb1        98G   24K   93G   1% /rsync
Resizes the file system to use the extended partition space. 
resize2fs /dev/sdb1
Checks the updated file system disk space usage. 
Filesystem      Size  Used Avail Use% Mounted on
/dev/sdb1       108G   24K  103G   1% /rsync

docs.title: ISCSI
docs.text: This subsection covers the installation and management of iSCSI targets.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
Installs the necessary packages for iSCSI. 

apt -y install open-iscsi lsscsi

docs.title: list
docs.text:
Discovers available iSCSI targets from the specified IP/DNS. 

iscsiadm -m discovery -t sendtargets -p ${IP/DNS}

docs.title: connect
docs.text:
Connects to the discovered iSCSI targets. 

iscsiadm -m node --login

docs.title: show
docs.text:
Shows the current iSCSI sessions and lists SCSI devices. 

iscsiadm -m session -o show
lsscsi

docs.title: samba/cifs
docs.text:
apt install  samba-common smbclient samba-common-bin smbclient  cifs-utils

vi /root/.rasp
user=SMBusername
password=SMBpassword

//192.168.1.10/SMB_share_name   /mnt/nas      cifs    uid=0,credentials=/root/.rasp,iocharset=utf8,vers=3.0,noperm,noserverino,nofail  0 0

docs.text:
$ passwd
Changing password for user cyklodev.
Current password:
New password:
Retype new password:
passwd: all authentication tokens updated successfully.

docs.title: get password age
docs.text:
$ chage -l cyklodev
Last password change                                    : Sep 30, 2023
Password expires                                        : never
Password inactive                                       : never
Account expires                                         : never
Minimum number of days between password change          : 0
Maximum number of days between password change          : 99999
Number of days of warning before password expires       : 7

docs.title: change age password
docs.text:
# chage -I -1 -m 1 -M 90 -E -1 -d $(date '+%F') cyklodev
  • -I : day before account is locked
  • -m : minimal duration before password can be changed
  • -M : maximum duration of valid password
  • -E : date of password expiration (days from timestamp init)
  • -d : date of password changing

docs.text:
apt -y install apt-transport-https wget gnupg
wget -O - https://packages.icinga.com/icinga.key | gpg --dearmor -o /usr/share/keyrings/icinga-archive-keyring.gpg
DIST=$(awk -F"[)(]+" '/VERSION=/ {print $2}' /etc/os-release); \
echo "deb [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/icinga-archive-keyring.gpg] https://packages.icinga.com/debian icinga-${DIST} main" > \
/etc/apt/sources.list.d/${DIST}-icinga.list
echo "deb-src [signed-by=/usr/share/keyrings/icinga-archive-keyring.gpg] https://packages.icinga.com/debian icinga-${DIST} main" >> \
/etc/apt/sources.list.d/${DIST}-icinga.list

docs.title: Node
docs.text:
• Agent/Satellite : Y

  • Parent zone : master

  • Bind Port : default
  • Parent config : Y
  • Parent command : Y
  • Local Zone: default
  • Parent Zone: default
  • Additionnal zone : N
  • Disable local conf : Y

docs.title: apply

firewall-cmd --reload

docs.title: Ipv4
docs.text: This section explains how to manage IPv4 firewall rules.

docs.title: add
docs.text:
Adds a firewall rule to allow traffic on port 80 (HTTP) and port 53 (DNS) for IPv4. 

firewall-cmd --add-port=80/tcp --permanent
firewall-cmd --add-port=53/udp --permanent

docs.title: Ipv6
docs.text: This section explains how to manage IPv6 firewall rules.

docs.title: add
docs.text:
Adds firewall rules to allow traffic on port 80 (HTTP) and port 443 (HTTPS) for IPv6. 

firewall-cmd --permanent --add-rich-rule='rule family=ipv6 port port=80 protocol=tcp accept'
firewall-cmd --permanent --add-rich-rule='rule family=ipv6 port port=443 protocol=tcp accept'

docs.title: remove
docs.text:
Removes firewall rules that allow traffic on port 443 (HTTPS) for IPv6. 

firewall-cmd --permanent --remove-rich-rule='rule family=ipv6 protocol=tcp port=443'
firewall-cmd --permanent --remove-rich-rule='rule family=ipv6 protocol=tcp port=443'

docs.title: Direct edit
docs.text:
vi /etc/firewalld/zones/public.xml
service firewalld reload

docs.title: List
docs.text:
alias iptlist='iptables -L -n -v --line-numbers'
alias iptlistin='iptables -L INPUT -n -v --line-numbers'
alias iptlistout='iptables -L OUTPUT -n -v --line-numbers'
alias iptlistfw='iptables -L FORWARD -n -v --line-numbers'
alias iptlistnat='iptables -t nat -v -L -n --line-number'

docs.title: Save / Restore
docs.text:
alias iptrestore='iptables-restore < /usr/local/sbin/iptables'
alias iptsave='iptables-save > /usr/local/sbin/iptables'

docs.title: Append
docs.text: iptables -A FORWARD -t filter -s 192.168.1.0/24 -j ACCEPT -o eth0

docs.title: Insert (position 1)
docs.text: iptables -I FORWARD 1 -m state -s 192.168.2.0/24 -d 192.168.77.0/24 --state NEW,RELATED,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT

docs.title: by specification
docs.text:
iptables -S
iptables -D [specification]

docs.title: by line number
docs.text:
iptables -L --line-numbers
iptables -D INPUT 5

docs.title: PAT
docs.text:
iptables -t nat -A PREROUTING -p tcp --dport 27027 -j DNAT --to-destination 192.168.77.20:27017
iptables -t nat -A PREROUTING -p tcp -s XX.XX.XX.XX/32 --dport 27027 -j DNAT --to-destination 192.168.77.20:27017 -m comment --comment "Mongo DB direct access"

docs.title: Reset
docs.text:
iptables -P INPUT ACCEPT
iptables -P FORWARD ACCEPT
iptables -P OUTPUT ACCEPT
iptables -t mangle -F
iptables -t nat -F
iptables -X
iptables -F

docs.title: Generate
docs.text: This section provides commands for generating and managing SSL certificates using Certbot and Lego.

docs.title: certbot
docs.text: This subsection explains how to use Certbot for certificate generation, renewal, and deletion.

docs.title: generate
docs.text:
Generates a new SSL certificate using Certbot with a dry run to test, followed by the actual generation. 

certbot certonly --agree-tos --email your@email.dom --webroot -w /var/lib/letsencrypt/ -d vhost.email.dom --dry-run
certbot certonly --agree-tos --email your@email.dom --webroot -w /var/lib/letsencrypt/ -d vhost.email.dom

docs.title: renew
docs.text:
Renews all the SSL certificates managed by Certbot. 

certbot renew

docs.title: delete
docs.text:
Deletes a specific SSL certificate using Certbot. 

certbot delete --cert-name vhost.email.dom

docs.title: lego
docs.text: This subsection explains how to use Lego for certificate generation and management with various DNS providers.

docs.title: install
docs.text:
Installs Lego by downloading the latest release and extracting it. 

wget https://github.com/go-acme/lego/releases/download/v4.16.1/lego_v4.16.1_linux_amd64.tar.gz
tar xvzf lego_v4.16.1_linux_amd64.tar.gz
./lego

docs.title: providers
docs.text:
Provides a link to the documentation for configuring DNS providers with Lego. 

https://go-acme.github.io/lego/dns/

docs.title: OVH
docs.text:
Shows how to configure and use Lego with the OVH DNS provider. 

export OVH_APPLICATION_KEY=xxxxxxxxxxx
export OVH_APPLICATION_SECRET=xxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx 
export OVH_CONSUMER_KEY=xxxxxxxxxxxxxx
export OVH_ENDPOINT=ovh-eu

./lego --email your@email.dom --dns ovh --domains vhost.email.dom run
ls -l .lego/certificates/vhost.email.dom.{crt,key}
./lego --email your@email.dom --dns ovh --domains *.email.dom run
ls -l .lego/certificates/_.email.dom.{crt,key}

docs.title: Read certificate
docs.text: openssl x509 -text -noout -in certificate.crt

docs.title: generate
docs.text: ssh-keygen -t ed25519

docs.title: list
docs.text:
ls -l ~/.ssh/
total 40
-rw-------. 1 clinux clinux   508 Dec 13 14:21 authorized_keys      # list of authorized public keys
-rw-r--r--. 1 clinux clinux    49 May 16 13:58 config               # configuration (ex ssh jump)
-rw-------. 1 clinux clinux   411 Oct 20  2023 id_ed25519           # Keep this private 
-rw-r--r--. 1 clinux clinux   101 Oct 20  2023 id_ed25519.pub       # used to authenticate

docs.title: New NIC NAT
docs.text:
vi /etc/network/interfaces

auto vmbr2
iface vmbr2 inet static
    address  192.168.77.1
    netmask  255.255.255.0
    bridge_ports none
    bridge_stp off
    bridge_fd 0
    post-up echo 1 > /proc/sys/net/ipv4/ip_forward
    post-up   iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -s '192.168.77.0/24' -o vmbr0 -j MASQUERADE
    post-down iptables -t nat -D POSTROUTING -s '192.168.77.0/24' -o vmbr0 -j MASQUERADE

docs.title: SSH Jump
docs.text: ssh -J jumper@proxmox user@vm

docs.title: SSH config jump
docs.text:
Host jumper-proxmox
HostName proxmox
User jumper

Host vm-behind-nat-rebuild
HostName vm-nated-ip
ProxyJump jumper-proxmox
User root
IdentityFile ~/.ssh/id_ed25519

docs.title: PAT ssh to VM
docs.text: iptables -t nat -A PREROUTING -p tcp --dport 122 -j DNAT --to-destination 192.168.77.121:22

docs.title: get qcow image
docs.text: cd /tmp && wget https://cloud.debian.org/images/cloud/bookworm/20231013-1532/debian-12-genericcloud-amd64-20231013-1532.qcow2

docs.title: build template vm
docs.text:
qm create 1000 --memory 1024 --core 1 --name debian12-temp --net0 virtio,bridge=vmbr0 --description "Debian 12 cloud template"
qm importdisk 1000 /tmp/debian-12-genericcloud-amd64-20231013-1532.qcow2 local-lvm
qm set 1000 --scsihw virtio-scsi-pci --scsi0 local-lvm:vm-1000-disk-0
qm set 1000 --boot c --bootdisk scsi0
qm set 1000 --ide2 local-lvm:cloudinit
qm set 1000 --serial0 socket --vga serial0
rm -f /tmp/debian-12-genericcloud-amd64-20231013-1532.qcow2

docs.title: Gui
docs.text: VM > Hardware > HD > Disk Action > Resize

docs.title: Cli
docs.text: qm resize VMID DISKNAME +5G

docs.title: Guest
docs.text:
growpart /dev/sda 1
resize2fs /dev/sda1

docs.title: List storage
docs.text: # pvesm status

docs.title: List disk in storage
docs.text: # pvesm list local

docs.title: List disk in storage
docs.text: # pvesm free STORAGE:DISK

docs.title: List vm
docs.text: # qm list

docs.title: List disk
docs.text: # qm config $VMID

docs.title: Remove disk
docs.text: # qm set $VMID --delete unused0

docs.title: Create VM
docs.text:
qm create 200 \
    --memory 4096 \
    --core 3 --cpu "cputype=x86-64-v2-AES" \
    --onboot 1 --ostype l26 \
    --name vm-cli \
    --description "VM via qm" \
    --net0 virtio,bridge=vmbr0 \
    --scsihw virtio-scsi-single
qm set 200 --ide2  local:iso/Rocky-9.3-x86_64-minimal.iso,media=cdrom
qm set 200 --scsi0 local-lvm:10,format=qcow2
qm set 200 --boot order='scsi0;ide2;net0'

docs.title: Delete vm
docs.text:
qm stop 200
qm destroy 200

docs.title: Snapshot vm
docs.text:
qm snapshot 200 test_snap
qm snapshot 200 test_snap_with_ram --vmstate 1
qm listsnapshot 200
qm delsnapshot 200 test_snap

docs.title: Rollback vm
docs.text:
qm rollback 200 test_snap
qm rollback 200 test_snap --start 1
qm rollback 200 test_snap_with_ram

docs.title: free page
docs.text:
free -m
echo 1 > /proc/sys/vm/compact_memory
free -m

docs.title: Gui
docs.text: Attention la version du protocole est 3 Datacenter > Storage > SMB/CIFS

docs.title: Cli
docs.text: pvesm add cifs syno --server $(IP/DNS) --share $(SHARE NAME) --username $(USERNAME) --password $(PASSWORD)

docs.title: Install
docs.text: Rocky9 installation

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Organization : proxmox

    Bucket : proxmox

    Save  $TOKEN

docs.title: Gui
docs.text:
datacenter > Metric Server > Add > InfluxDB

    Name : metrics
    Server : IP/FQDN
    Port : 8086
    Protocol : HTTP
    Token : $TOKEN

docs.title: Install
docs.text: Rocky9 installation

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
datasource > add datasource > influxdb
- Protocol Flux - Server : localhost 


    Save  $TOKEN
